mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2018-11-15 indicates easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods (FEDFUNDS < 2.0%) are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 persons
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and forward-looking recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the correlation between joint RSAFS/HOUST weakness and subsequent equity returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) falls below 98.0 (2017=100), indicating manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness that reduces corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO < 98.0 are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, signaling labor market deterioration that increases recession probability and triggers equity de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% as of 2018-11-15 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or post-shock recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 70% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%, creating a negative real interest rate regime that compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation above target
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in negative real rate regimes are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% with t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized, signaling weakening economic momentum that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDP deceleration below 2.0% are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 800 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) growth slows below 2% year-over-year, jointly signaling consumer demand weakness that precedes equity market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 800 thousand AND RSAFS year-over-year growth < 2%
regime_hint: consumer demand weakness in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or the joint interaction effect on forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
